summary: Phoenix Shield Mold Removal business management app with AI-powered social media manager and smart CRM/operations system for mold remediation company in Tullahoma, TN - now featuring official Phoenix Shield branding with logo, company mascot character, and brand color scheme (dark blue #1a3a52, lime green #b8d430)
architectural_notes: Internet Identity authentication for secure single-user access
Mobile-friendly responsive design for on-the-go business management
Template-based AI content generation system for social media (no external AI API integration)
Local storage for customer data, job history, and business metrics
Estimates stored as standalone records with optional customer association for flexibility
Follow-up and review request messages are generated as copy-ready text for manual sending through existing communication channels (no automatic sending integration)
Phoenix Shield brand assets (logo, mascot character) integrated as visual elements throughout the app
Brand color scheme uses dark blue (#1a3a52) as primary, lime green (#b8d430) as accent, with black/white supporting colors
Phoenix Shield logo assets (logo-1.jpg primary black/white version, logo-2.jpg colorful version) stored in frontend/public/assets and referenced as static assets throughout the application

existing_features.title: Create a customer database that stores customer information including name, phone, email, address, and service history with the ability to add, edit, view, and delete customer records
Implement a job history timeline for each customer that displays past services, dates, and issues found in chronological order
Build an itemized estimate calculator that allows input of square footage, materials, and labor hours with line items that automatically calculate subtotals and a final total
Create a photo gallery system for each customer that allows uploading and organizing before/after photos of mold remediation work
Implement a scheduling calendar with drag-and-drop functionality that allows creating, moving, and managing appointments for mold remediation jobs
Build a job status tracking system with three states: scheduled, in-progress, and completed, with the ability to update job status and view jobs filtered by status
Create a follow-up reminder system that allows setting reminders for preventive check-ins with customers at specified intervals (such as 6-month mold prevention check-ins)
Implement a customer communication log that records all calls, texts, and emails with timestamps and notes for each customer
Build a revenue tracking dashboard that displays monthly business insights including total revenue, number of jobs completed, and average job value

Create an AI-powered social media post generator using intelligent templates and frameworks specifically for mold remediation content that generates engaging posts based on user-selected topic, tone, and target platform

Build a template library browser for mold remediation social media content with pre-defined categories including before/after transformations, mold prevention tips, customer testimonials, emergency response, and seasonal content that users can browse, customize, and use
Implement a content calendar for planning social media posts that displays posts in a monthly calendar view and allows scheduling posts for future dates

Create a post preview and editing system that displays generated or template-based social media posts with the ability to edit content and copy to clipboard for manual posting to social media platforms
Implement Internet Identity authentication so only the business owner can securely access all business data, customer information, and management features

Design a mobile-friendly, clean user interface with a cohesive visual theme that uses warm, trustworthy colors (earth tones like greens and browns instead of blue/purple) to reflect environmental remediation and safety, with clear typography and intuitive navigation suitable for on-the-go business management
Implement frontend customer management interface with forms to add new customers, edit existing customer information, view customer details, and delete customer records

Implement a job history timeline component in the frontend that displays all past services for a selected customer in chronological order, showing service dates, types of work performed, and issues discovered
Create a calendar interface in the frontend that displays scheduled appointments in a monthly view with the ability to create new appointments, view appointment details, and navigate between months
Create a job status management interface in the frontend that displays jobs grouped or filtered by status with the ability to update job status and view status-specific job lists
Integrate Internet Identity authentication so that all customer data, job information, and business features are only accessible to authenticated users

Design and implement a mobile-responsive user interface with an earth tone color scheme using warm greens and browns, clear typography optimized for readability, intuitive navigation suitable for on-the-go business management, and consistent spacing and layout patterns across all pages

Add navigation menu items for Dashboard and Social Media sections to the Header component navigation, displaying them alongside existing Customers, Calendar, and Jobs links in both desktop and mobile views
Create a Dashboard page component that displays revenue insights including monthly total revenue, number of jobs completed, and average job value
Create a Social Media page component with tabs or sections for AI post generator, content calendar, template library, and post preview/editing functionality
Configure routing to support Dashboard and Social Media pages with appropriate route paths and navigation integration
Add an 'Estimates' navigation menu item to the Header component that appears alongside existing Customers, Calendar, Jobs, Dashboard, and Social Media links in both desktop and mobile views
Create an EstimatesPage component that displays a calculator form interface for creating itemized estimates
Implement a square footage input section in the calculator with fields for square footage amount and price per square foot that displays a calculated subtotal

Implement a materials section in the calculator that allows adding multiple line items, each with fields for material name, quantity, and unit cost, displaying a subtotal for each line item and a total for all materials
Implement a labor section in the calculator with fields for hours worked and hourly rate that displays a calculated labor subtotal
Display a grand total in the calculator that automatically sums the square footage subtotal, all materials subtotals, and labor subtotal
Configure routing to support the Estimates page with an appropriate route path and integrate it with the application's router configuration

Create React Query hooks for estimate CRUD operations including useEstimates to fetch all estimates, useCreateEstimate to save new estimates, useUpdateEstimate to modify existing estimates, and useDeleteEstimate to remove estimates

Modify the EstimatesPage component to add a 'Save Estimate' button that persists the current calculator state as a new estimate record, and a 'Load Estimate' dropdown or button that allows selecting and loading a previously saved estimate into the calculator for editing

Create a new SavedEstimatesPage component that displays a list of all saved estimates showing key details (creation date, square footage, total amount) with buttons to edit, delete, or copy each estimate
Add a 'Saved Estimates' navigation menu item to the Header component that appears alongside existing navigation links in both desktop and mobile views
Configure routing to support the SavedEstimatesPage with an appropriate route path and integrate it with the application's router configuration in App.tsx

Add a section to the CustomerDetailPage that displays a list of saved estimates with an option to associate estimates with the current customer, showing estimate summaries and copy-to-clipboard functionality

Implement a copy-to-clipboard function that formats an estimate as readable text including all line items, subtotals, and the grand total, and displays a success notification when the copy operation completes
Add a 'Create Job' button to the JobsPage component that opens a form dialog for creating new job records

Implement a job creation form with fields for customer selection (dropdown populated from existing customers), service type (text input), description (textarea), scheduled date (date picker), and initial status (dropdown with options: scheduled, in-progress, completed)
Wire the job creation form to submit data using the useCreateJob mutation hook, display success feedback, close the dialog on successful creation, and show error messages if creation fails

Create React Query hooks for follow-up reminder operations including useFollowUpReminders to fetch all reminders, useCreateFollowUpReminder to create new reminders, useUpdateFollowUpReminder to modify reminder templates, and useCompleteFollowUpReminder to mark reminders as sent

Create a FollowUpsPage component that displays upcoming and past follow-up reminders in a list or timeline view with the ability to filter by reminder type (post-job satisfaction, 6-month mold prevention, seasonal check-in) and view reminder details

Implement a follow-up reminder template editor dialog that displays the current message template for a selected reminder with a text area for editing the template, preview of the formatted message, and a copy-to-clipboard button to copy the final message for manual sending

Create pre-defined message templates for each follow-up type: post-job satisfaction (requesting feedback 3-7 days after job completion), 6-month mold prevention (preventive check-in reminder), and seasonal check-ins (spring moisture season, fall prep) that users can customize before sending

Add automatic follow-up reminder creation when a job is marked as completed that creates a post-job satisfaction reminder scheduled for 3 days after completion and a 6-month mold prevention reminder scheduled for 6 months after completion

Create a Google review request message generator component that displays an editable template asking customers to leave a Google review with a placeholder for the business Google review link, preview of the formatted message, and copy-to-clipboard functionality for manual sending
Add a 'Request Review' button to the CustomerDetailPage and JobCard components that opens the Google review request generator dialog pre-populated with the customer's name in the message template
Add a 'Follow-Ups' navigation menu item to the Header component that appears alongside existing navigation links in both desktop and mobile views
Configure routing to support the FollowUpsPage with an appropriate route path (/follow-ups) and integrate it with the application's router configuration in App.tsx

Add a 'Follow-Ups' navigation menu item to the Header component that appears in the desktop navigation menu alongside existing Customers, Calendar, Jobs, Dashboard, Social Media, Estimates, and Saved Estimates links
Add a 'Follow-Ups' navigation menu item to the mobile sheet menu in the Header component that appears alongside existing navigation options in the mobile view
Fix the Follow-Ups page routing and component configuration to resolve the 'Not Found' message that appears in the top left when accessing the page through navigation
Redesign the FollowUpsPage component to display message templates as the primary content, replacing the current customer list view with a template-focused layout

Display all four message template types simultaneously on the Follow-Ups page: post-job satisfaction (3-7 days after completion), 6-month mold prevention check-in, seasonal check-ins (spring moisture season and fall prep), and Google review request template
Implement editable text areas for each message template on the Follow-Ups page that allow users to customize the template content in real-time
Add a preview section for each message template that displays the formatted message with customer name placeholders and shows how the message will appear when sent
Add a copy-to-clipboard button for each message template that copies the formatted message text and displays a success notification when the copy operation completes
Create default message template text for post-job satisfaction that requests customer feedback 3-7 days after job completion, including placeholders for customer name and customizable content

Create default message template text for 6-month mold prevention check-in that reminds customers about preventive maintenance and offers a check-in appointment, including placeholders for customer name

Create default message template text for seasonal check-ins covering spring moisture season and fall prep, with messaging appropriate for each season's mold prevention needs and customer name placeholders
Create default message template text for Google review request that politely asks customers to leave a review with a placeholder for the business Google review link and customer name
Replace all placeholder branding and logos throughout the application with the official Phoenix Shield logo (phoenix rising from shield with 'MOLD REMOVAL' text in circular badge)
Update the application color scheme from earth tones to Phoenix Shield brand colors using dark blue (#1a3a52) as primary, lime green (#b8d430) as accent, with black and white for contrast
Incorporate the Phoenix Shield mascot character (friendly professional in green polo shirt) as visual elements on the DashboardPage to add personality and brand recognition
Add the Phoenix Shield mascot character to the SocialMediaPage as a visual element that reinforces brand identity in the social media management section
Update the LoginPage hero section to feature Phoenix Shield branding with the company colors, logo, and professional messaging that reflects the mold removal business identity

Update the Header component background, text colors, and styling to use Phoenix Shield brand colors (dark blue #1a3a52 background, lime green #b8d430 accents) for consistent brand identity across all pages

Add the Phoenix Shield mascot character image (character.png - bald head, beard, friendly smile, green polo with Phoenix Shield logo, crossed arms pose) to the DashboardPage component as a visual element that reinforces brand personality and recognition
Add the Phoenix Shield mascot character image (character.png) to the SocialMediaPage component as a visual element that reinforces brand identity in the social media management section
Replace the placeholder logo in the Header component with the official Phoenix Shield logo (logo-1.jpg), updating the src path to point to /assets/logo-1.jpg
Replace the placeholder logo in the LoginPage hero section with the official Phoenix Shield logo (logo-1.jpg), updating the image source to /assets/logo-1.jpg
Add a search input field to the CustomersPage component that filters the displayed customer list in real-time as the user types, matching against customer name, phone number, email, or address fields

Add a 'Download PDF' or 'Export as PDF' button to the EstimatesPage component that generates a professionally formatted PDF document containing all estimate details including square footage breakdown, materials line items with quantities and costs, labor calculation, all subtotals, and the grand total

Add a 'Download PDF' or 'Export as PDF' button to each estimate card on the SavedEstimatesPage that generates a PDF for that specific saved estimate with the same professional formatting as the EstimatesPage PDF export
Add a 'Download PDF' or 'Export as PDF' button to the estimates section on the CustomerDetailPage that generates a PDF for the selected estimate associated with that customer

Verify the frontend CustomerForm component in frontend/src/components/customers/CustomerForm.tsx is correctly serializing and sending all customer data (name, phone, email, address) to the backend createCustomer mutation
Ensure the useCreateCustomer React Query mutation hook in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts properly handles the backend response and updates the customer list cache after successful creation

new_feature_requests.summary: AI-powered social media post generator with mold remediation templates
Content calendar for planning and scheduling posts
Template library for common scenarios (before/after, prevention tips, testimonials, seasonal content)
Post preview system with edit and copy to clipboard functionality
Customer database with complete job history timeline
Itemized estimate calculator with square footage, materials, labor hours, and automatic totals
Photo gallery per customer for before/after documentation
Drag-and-drop scheduling calendar with appointments
Job status tracking system (scheduled → in-progress → completed)
Follow-up reminder system for preventive check-ins
Customer communication log for calls, texts, emails
Revenue tracking dashboard with business insights
Create a customer database in the backend that stores customer records with fields for name, phone number, email address, physical address, and maintains a relationship to job history records
Create a job history data structure in the backend that stores job records linked to customers, including service date, service type, issues found, and job status
Build a scheduling system in the backend that stores appointment records with customer ID, scheduled date and time, job type, and duration
Implement a job status tracking system in the backend with three states (scheduled, in-progress, completed) and functions to update job status
Backend storage system for saved estimates with ability to save, retrieve, update, and delete estimate records
Saved Estimates page showing all saved estimates with edit and delete functionality
Integrate saved estimates view within customer profile pages
Copy-to-clipboard functionality for estimates to enable easy sharing via email or text

Create a backend data structure for saved estimates that stores estimate records as standalone entities (not tied to customers) with fields for estimate ID, creation date, square footage amount, square footage price per unit, materials array (each with name, quantity, unit cost), labor hours, labor hourly rate, and calculated totals
Implement backend functions to save new estimates, retrieve all estimates, retrieve a single estimate by ID, update an existing estimate, and delete an estimate
Add 'Create Job' button and form to Jobs page to create new job records with customer selection, service type, description, scheduled date, and status
Create a useCreateJob React Query mutation hook that calls the backend createJob function to persist new job records
Enhanced follow-up system with multiple types: post-job satisfaction, 6-month mold prevention, seasonal check-ins with editable message templates and preview capability
Google review request message generator with editable template, preview capability, and copy to clipboard for manual sending to customers

Create a backend data structure for follow-up reminders that stores reminder type (post-job satisfaction, 6-month mold prevention, seasonal check-in), customer ID, job ID (optional), scheduled date, message template text, and completion status
Implement backend functions to create follow-up reminders, retrieve all reminders, retrieve reminders filtered by type or customer, update reminder templates, and mark reminders as completed
Update app color scheme from earth tones to Phoenix Shield brand colors (dark blue #1a3a52, lime green #b8d430, with black/white accents)
Incorporate Phoenix Shield mascot character as visual elements on dashboard, social media section, and other key pages
Store the uploaded mascot character image (character.png) in the frontend public assets directory (frontend/public/assets) so it can be referenced as a static asset throughout the application
Replace placeholder logo references with official Phoenix Shield logo files (logo-1.jpg and logo-2.jpg) throughout application including header, login page, and all brand touchpoints

Store the uploaded Phoenix Shield logo files (logo-1.jpg - black and white circular badge design, and image.png - colorful version with orange/red phoenix) in the frontend public assets directory at frontend/public/assets/
Update all other logo references throughout the application (if any exist in DashboardPage, SocialMediaPage, or other components) to use the official Phoenix Shield logo file path /assets/logo-1.jpg
Customer search and filter functionality on Customers page to find customers by name, phone, or address
Job filtering options on Jobs page to view jobs by status (scheduled, in-progress, completed)
Export estimates as PDF with professional formatting including all line items, subtotals, and grand total for download or email distribution

Implement filter buttons or a dropdown on the JobsPage that allows viewing jobs filtered by status: 'All Jobs', 'Scheduled', 'In Progress', and 'Completed', replacing or enhancing the existing tab-based filtering system

Debug and fix the backend createCustomer function in backend/main.mo to resolve the immediate persistence failure that occurs when submitting the customer creation form with name, phone, email, and address fields

projectName: Phoenix Shield Mold Removal Business App
